paper_title: Material and Methods of Sekuline Lakes/Gravel Pits Study

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and measurement of fossil/subfossil/recent remains for paleontological analysis. The specimens are prepared using a polyvinyl butyral solution and measured with a digital caliper for comparative analysis.

steps.description: The fossil/subfossil/recent remains were prepared with polyvinyl butyral (PVB) solution, in which the bones/teeth and antlers were submerged for 1 week, after which they were dried at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare specimens using polyvinyl butyral solution and dry them

steps.description: The specimens were measured by using a digital caliper with an accuracy of 0.05 mm.

methods.description: This method describes the process of radiocarbon dating performed on bone samples to determine their age. Collagen is extracted, converted to CO2, and then to benzene for 14C activity measurement.

steps.description: Collagen was extracted from the bone samples, burned to CO2, and then transferred to benzene.
steps.searchable_summary: extract collagen, convert to CO2, and transfer to benzene

steps.description: The 14C activity of benzene was measured by liquid scintillation counter (LSC; Quantulus 1220).
steps.searchable_summary: measure 14C activity using liquid scintillation counter
